identity.name: TutorIA
identity.design_philosophy: Dual-World Interface: A tactile, clay-morphism world for children vs. a clean, Swiss-style control room for parents.

headings.usage: All main titles in Child Dashboard. Use tight tracking.

child_ui.decorations: Use floating 3D shapes (CSS only or images) in background.

child_dashboard.container_style: Max width 1200px, centered, colorful background blobs.

parent_dashboard.container_style: Full width fluid container with sidebar navigation.

buttons.child_primary: bg-[#4CC9F0] text-black border-4 border-black rounded-full px-8 py-4 font-bold text-xl shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,1)] hover:translate-y-1 hover:shadow-none
buttons.child_secondary: bg-[#FFD60A] text-black border-4 border-black rounded-full px-6 py-3 font-bold text-lg shadow-[3px_3px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,1)] hover:translate-y-1 hover:shadow-none
buttons.parent_primary: bg-slate-900 text-white rounded-md px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium hover:bg-slate-800
buttons.parent_outline: border border-slate-200 bg-white hover:bg-slate-100 text-slate-900 rounded-md px-4 py-2 text-sm

cards.child_card: bg-white border-4 border-black rounded-3xl p-6 shadow-[6px_6px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,0.1)]
cards.parent_card: bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-lg p-6 shadow-sm

accessibility.contrast: Ensure all text on colored backgrounds (child theme) is strictly #000000 or #FFFFFF depending on APCA score.
accessibility.text_size: Minimum 16px for body text in child view.

instructions_to_main_agent: DETECT USER TYPE: Always check if the current route is '/child' or '/parent' to apply the correct theme.
USE LUCIDE ICONS: For the child UI, use Lucide icons with 'stroke-width={3}' and wrap them in colorful circles.
NO GRADIENTS FOR KIDS: Use solid, flat colors with thick black borders for that 'cartoon' look.
CHART LIBRARY: Use 'recharts' for parent dashboards. Keep them simple (Bar, Line, Pie).
DATA-TESTID: Every button and input MUST have a data-testid attribute.
universal_guidelines: Do not use generic centered layouts.
Create depth through layered design elements.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable.
